task.name: Replace local formatCurrency with shared utility and fix token usage
task.action: In `src/components/dashboard/AccountsSummary.tsx`: 1. Remove the local `formatCurrency` function (lines 26-31). 2. Add import: `import { formatCurrency } from '@/lib/format'`. 3. In `CreditCardItem`, replace `text-white` with `text-foreground` on the balance div. 4. Convert `export default AccountsSummary` to `export { AccountsSummary }`. 5. Update any consumer that imports AccountsSummary to use the named import (check `src/app/` pages). 6. Deduplicate `SkeletonAccountList` and `SkeletonCardList` into a single `SkeletonList` component since they are identical.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — zero errors. Grep for the old `export default AccountsSummary` to confirm it's gone. Verify the dashboard page still imports correctly.

task.name: Upgrade error state to match TotalNetWorthCard pattern
task.action: In `AccountsSummary.tsx`: 1. Import `AlertCircle` from `lucide-react`. 2. Update `ErrorState` to accept an `onRetry` callback prop. 3. Render: AlertCircle icon (h-8 w-8, text-error-600), error title ("Failed to load accounts" / "Failed to load credit cards"), error detail text, and a "Try again" button that calls `onRetry()`. 4. Style the retry button: `cursor-pointer text-sm font-medium text-primary hover:text-primary/80 transition-colors mt-1`. 5. In `TopAccounts`, destructure `refetch` from `useAccountsQuery()` and pass it to ErrorState as `onRetry`. 6. In `TopCreditCards`, destructure `refetch` from `useCardsQuery()` and pass it to ErrorState as `onRetry`.
task.verify: Temporarily force an error state (e.g., disconnect network) and confirm the AlertCircle icon, error message, and "Try again" button render. Confirm clicking "Try again" triggers a refetch. Run `npm run lint`.

task.name: Enhance empty state with contextual icons
task.action: In `AccountsSummary.tsx`: 1. Import `Wallet` and `CreditCard` icons from `lucide-react`. 2. Update `EmptyState` to render a contextual icon above the text: - Accounts: `Wallet` (h-8 w-8, text-muted-foreground/50) - Cards: `CreditCard` (h-8 w-8, text-muted-foreground/50) 3. Add `gap-2` to the empty state container for spacing between icon and text.
task.verify: If possible, test with an account/card-less state (or temporarily override data). Confirm icons render. Run `npm run build`.

task.name: Add account type icons and make items clickable links
task.action: In `AccountsSummary.tsx`: 1. Import icons from `lucide-react`: `Landmark`, `PiggyBank`, `TrendingUp`, `Banknote`, `Bitcoin`, `Clock`, `Home`, `Briefcase`, `Smartphone`, `Wallet`, `CreditCard`. 2. Create a `getAccountTypeIcon` helper that maps account type strings to their icon components (see spec for mapping). 3. Update `AccountItem` to: a. Accept an `id` prop. b. Render the account type icon (h-4 w-4, text-muted-foreground) to the left of the name/type text. c. Wrap the entire row in `Link` from `next/link` pointing to `/accounts/{id}`. d. Add hover classes: `flex items-center justify-between rounded-lg px-2 -mx-2 py-2 hover:bg-secondary-500/10 transition-colors duration-200 cursor-pointer`. 4. Update `CreditCardItem` to: a. Accept an `id` prop. b. Render a `CreditCard` icon (h-4 w-4, text-muted-foreground) to the left of the name. c. Wrap the entire row in `Link` pointing to `/cards/{id}`. d. Add the same hover classes as AccountItem. 5. Pass `account.id` and `card.id` from the parent map functions.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually confirm icons render for each account type. Hover over items — confirm background change and cursor pointer. Click an item — confirm navigation to `/accounts/{id}` or `/cards/{id}`.

task.name: Add staggered entrance animations with reduced-motion support
task.action: In `AccountsSummary.tsx`: 1. Import `motion` and `useReducedMotion` from `framer-motion`. 2. In both `TopAccounts` and `TopCreditCards`: a. Call `const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion()`. b. Wrap each list item's `motion.div` (or use `motion(Link)`) with: - `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}` - `animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` - `transition={{ duration: 0.3, ease: "easeOut", delay: index * 0.05 }}` c. When `prefersReducedMotion` is true, skip the motion props entirely (render plain elements or set `initial` and `animate` to the same values). 3. Keep the "See All" button and section header static — only animate list items.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Reload the dashboard — confirm list items fade/slide in with a stagger effect. Enable "Reduce motion" in OS accessibility settings — confirm items appear instantly without animation. Run `npm run lint`.
